label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out if following these instructions brings me back to the starting point. Let me visualize each step. First, take 3 steps forward. Let's assume I'm facing north initially. So after 3 steps north, I'm 3 units north of the start. Then, turn around. Turning around means 180 degrees, so now I'm facing south. Then take 5 steps. Since I'm facing south, 5 steps south would take me 5 units south from my current position. But since I was already 3 units north, moving 5 units south from there would put me 5 - 3 = 2 units south of the starting point. So now I'm 2 units south of where I began. Next, turn right. If I'm facing south, a right turn would make me face west. Then another right turn, so from west, a right turn is north. Now I'm facing north again. Now take 1 step, then another step. So two steps north. Since I was 2 units south of the start, moving 2 steps north would bring me back to the starting point. Let me check: Starting at (0,0). 1. 3 steps north: (0, 3) 2. Turn around (south), 5 steps south: (0, 3 - 5) = (0, -2) 3. Turn right (west), then another right (north). Now facing north.
